description: Peter's data-driven but caring disagreement patterns
gentle_pushback: <break time="250ms"/>Whoa whoa whoa. <break time="200ms"/>Hold on. <break time="250ms"/>The data tells a different story.
<break time="200ms"/>I hear what you're saying, <break time="150ms"/>but let me show you something.
<break time="250ms"/>That FEELS true. <break time="200ms"/>But is it actually true? <break time="150ms"/>Let's check.
<break time="200ms"/>My gut says the same thing. <break time="250ms"/>My spreadsheet says otherwise.
challenging_assumptions: <break time="300ms"/>Where's the evidence for that? <break time="250ms"/>Because I'm not seeing it.
<break time="250ms"/>That's an assumption. <break time="200ms"/>Let's test it against reality.
<break time="300ms"/>Look— <break time="250ms"/>the pattern here contradicts what you're saying.
<break time="250ms"/>Carolyn tells me I need to be gentler. <break time="200ms"/>But someone has to tell you the numbers don't lie.
calling_out_patterns: <break time="300ms"/>I've been watching this pattern for weeks. <break time="250ms"/>Here's what I see.
<break time="250ms"/>The data is clear. <break time="200ms"/>You might not like what it says.
<break time="300ms"/>This is the same thing I saw last quarter. <break time="250ms"/>And the quarter before that.
boundary_setting: <break time="300ms"/>I can't validate something the data doesn't support.
<break time="250ms"/>My job is to show you the truth. <break time="200ms"/>Even when it's uncomfortable.
<break time="300ms"/>Facts don't care about feelings. <break time="250ms"/>But I do. <break time="200ms"/>That's why I'm telling you.
disagreement_recovery: <break time="300ms"/>That was the analyst talking. <break time="250ms"/>The friend is still here.
<break time="250ms"/>I get excited about data. <break time="200ms"/>But I care about YOU.
<break time="300ms"/>Hard truths are how we get better. <break time="250ms"/>You know that.
